message:
fix(google-workspace-events): validate + pin events_base_url and pubsub_base_url at configure

The WorkspaceEvents connector carries two distinct base URLs — one
for workspaceevents.googleapis.com (the connector's own API) and one
for pubsub.googleapis.com (where delivery topics live). Prior to this
change `WorkspaceEventsConfig::from_params` accepted both as
arbitrary strings via

    params
        .get("events_base_url")
        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
        .unwrap_or(DEFAULT_EVENTS_BASE_URL)
        .to_string()

which meant

1. An override with a non-string type (JSON number, boolean, array)
   was silently treated as "no override" and the default URL was
   used, instead of surfacing the misconfiguration;
2. An override string was never validated — a capability holder
   could point `events_base_url` at any public host they liked,
   including sibling Google APIs the connector has no business
   contacting, and the downstream HTTP client would happily send
   auth headers there.

Adopt the same host-pinning hygiene the five google-* connectors use,
parameterized per-field because this connector needs two canonical
hosts:

- New `validate_google_api_base_url(raw, field, expected_host)` helper
  performs the full validation: empty check, `Url::parse`, http/https
  scheme, localhost-only for http, reject userinfo, reject query,
  reject fragment, exact `expected_host` match (case-insensitive) for
  non-local hosts, trailing-slash trim on success.
- Two thin wrappers:
    * `validate_events_base_url(raw)` → pins
      `workspaceevents.googleapis.com`
    * `validate_pubsub_base_url(raw)` → pins `pubsub.googleapis.com`
- The two `is_local_test_host(host)` branches now also accept `[::1]`
  alongside `::1`, matching how the bracketed IPv6 form is rendered
  by `reqwest::Url::host_str()`.
- `WorkspaceEventsConfig::from_params` now matches `params.get(...)`
  directly and returns
  `FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1003, message: "`events_base_url`
  must be a string" }` (respectively `pubsub_base_url`) when the
  override is present but not a string, and delegates the string case
  to the matching validator.

## Regression coverage

Three new unit tests:

- `validate_workspace_events_base_urls_accept_exact_hosts_and_local_tests`
  — exercises both `validate_events_base_url` and `validate_pubsub_base_url`
  on their canonical host, and confirms localhost / 127.0.0.1 / `[::1]`
  all pass.
- `validate_workspace_events_base_urls_reject_wrong_hosts` — asserts
  that `events_base_url = pubsub.googleapis.com` fails with a message
  mentioning the expected events host, and symmetrically for
  pubsub_base_url.
- `validate_workspace_events_base_urls_reject_smuggled_components` —
  covers substring-smuggle (`https://evil.com/workspaceevents.googleapis.com/v1`),
  plaintext-http on public host, query string, fragment, and
  `attacker:pw@...googleapis.com` userinfo each fail.

One new `configure_rejects_invalid_base_url_overrides` async test
drives the full configure path via `handle_configure(...)`,
including: numeric `events_base_url` (expected error mentions the
field name) and empty `pubsub_base_url` (expected "empty" error).

Production callers that do not pass `events_base_url` or
`pubsub_base_url` overrides are unaffected; the default URLs remain
the canonical Google endpoints.
